name: Omelette with Zucchini Tomato and Basil
recipeIngredient: 1 tomato, medium (peeled, seeded, and coarsely chopped)
2 tablespoons fresh basil leaves, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------------OMELETTES---------------------------------
12 to 18 eggs (2 to 3 per person), plus a pinch of salt and pepper
½ tablespoon club soda
6 tablespoons unsalted butter (¾ stick)
Peeled, seeded, and coarsely chopped tomato
1. TO MAKE THE FILLING: In a medium skillet, melt the butter over medium heat until it begins to shimmer. Add the minced shallot and sauté for about 2 minutes until soft. Add the sliced mushrooms and continue to sauté another 2 minutes. Add the julienned zucchini and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
2. Add the chopped tomato, salt, pepper, and basil; stir well. Season with additional salt and pepper if needed. Set aside and keep warm.
3. TO PREPARE EACH OMELETTE: In a bowl, whisk together 2 to 3 eggs with a pinch of salt, a pinch of black pepper, and the club soda until smooth.
4. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in an 8-inch omelette pan or skillet over medium heat until it begins to sizzle.
5. Pour in the egg mixture and gently stir with the flat side of a fork in the center of the pan. Use the fork prongs to lift the edges, allowing any uncooked egg to slide toward the edge of the pan.
6. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the omelette is set but still slightly runny on top. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of the prepared filling onto one half of the omelette nearest the handle. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon Parmesan cheese over the filling.
7. Quickly and gently flip the pan so that the other half of the omelette folds over the filling. Slide the finished omelette onto a serving dish and serve immediately, or keep warm in a low oven while preparing additional omelettes.
Advance Preparation: The tomato-filling mixture may be prepared up to 1 day ahead without basil. Store refrigerated and remove from the refrigerator 30 minutes before using; add the basil and gently reheat.
